title: Boot-sector firmware provenance and segment string scan
findings: Cubie3 pre-partition strings show U-Boot 2018.07-12-boot-aw2501-gb2d229198b2-dirty built Jan 06 2026.
BL31 strings show v2.5(debug):5fc237a6a built 09:05:28 Feb 26 2025.
Early duplicate BOOT0/SCP strings appear near 0x37700 and 0x119700; U-Boot command/help strings begin around 0xc00000 to 0xcbffff.
Allwinner clock/devicetree-style SDMMC strings cluster around 0xd23xxx.
BL31 and SCP/ARISC strings cluster around 0xd3c000 to 0xd3fxxx.
ARISC/standby strings, including SMC clk glitch workaround in arisc, cluster around 0xd4c000.
No SMHC, IDMAC, firewall, TZMA, or SPC string appeared in the segment scan.
decision: No kernel patch. The capture now has boot firmware provenance and rough segment string map, but no concrete SDMMC0 IDMAC descriptor-fetch handoff bit.
next: If pursuing this path, compare the U-Boot aw2501 commit string/package payload or do bounded binary analysis around 0xd23xxx SDMMC clock-name tables; do not infer a Linux driver change from provenance alone.
